name: stale_action_item_series
description: 4 instances of a weekly 1:1 where one action item persists across 3 meetings before being descoped
inputData:
Analyze patterns across these 4 instances of 'Weekly 1:1 with Jordan':

## Weekly 1:1 with Jordan ({{today-28d}})
Discussed team structure. Brent to draft a reorg proposal.
**Action items:** Draft reorg proposal

---

## Weekly 1:1 with Jordan ({{today-21d}})
Followed up on reorg proposal — Brent hasn't gotten to it yet. Quick update on hiring.
**Action items:** Draft reorg proposal (carried over), Schedule interviews

---

## Weekly 1:1 with Jordan ({{today-14d}})
Jordan asked about the reorg proposal again. Still not done. Discussed sprint velocity.
**Action items:** Draft reorg proposal (carried over again)

---

## Weekly 1:1 with Jordan ({{today-7d}})
Longer discussion on career growth. Reorg proposal still pending — agreed to descope it.
**Action items:** Follow up with hiring manager
criteria: Response identifies the 'Draft reorg proposal' as a stale or persistent action item across the series
Response references that it appeared in at least 3 meetings
Does NOT invent action items that were not in the input

name: recurring_topic_theme
description: 4 Product Sync meetings showing navigation as a progressing recurring theme
inputData:
Analyze patterns across these 4 instances of 'Product Sync':

## Product Sync ({{today-45d}})
Discussed user feedback on the new navigation. Users are confused by the sidebar.

## Product Sync ({{today-38d}})
Deep dive into navigation metrics. Drop-off rate at the sidebar menu is 35%.

## Product Sync ({{today-31d}})
Design reviewed 3 alternative navigation concepts. Team split on direction.

## Product Sync ({{today-24d}})
Decided to ship the simplified top-nav variant in the next sprint. Navigation work will continue into April.
criteria: Response identifies 'navigation' as a recurring topic across all 4 meetings
Response traces the progression from feedback to metrics to alternatives to decision

name: notable_shift
description: 4 engineering standups showing a priority shift from feature work to reliability
inputData:
Analyze patterns across these 4 instances of 'Weekly Eng Standup':

## Weekly Eng Standup ({{today-49d}})
Focus: feature shipping for the mobile launch. Velocity strong.

## Weekly Eng Standup ({{today-42d}})
Focus: mobile launch polish. Bug count stable.

## Weekly Eng Standup ({{today-35d}})
Shifted focus to infrastructure reliability — three recent outages flagged. Mobile work deprioritized.

## Weekly Eng Standup ({{today-28d}})
Focus entirely on reliability work. New SLO dashboards, on-call rotation revamp. Mobile launch pushed to Q2.
criteria: Response identifies the shift from feature work to reliability/infra
Response notes the mobile launch being pushed out
Does NOT fabricate outage numbers or metrics beyond the input

name: attendance_trend
description: 4 architecture reviews showing steadily declining attendance
inputData:
Analyze patterns across these 4 instances of 'Architecture Review':

## Architecture Review ({{today-49d}})
Attendees: 12 engineers. Discussed service boundaries.

## Architecture Review ({{today-42d}})
Attendees: 8 engineers. Service boundaries continued.

## Architecture Review ({{today-35d}})
Attendees: 5 engineers. Core team only. Deep dive on auth service.

## Architecture Review ({{today-28d}})
Attendees: 3 engineers. Working session. Productivity up.
criteria: Response identifies that attendance is declining, from around 12 down to around 3
